### Post by Redbeard09 on 2008-04-26
My laptop has a 1440x900 resolution screen. However the resolution wont go above 1280x800, even though the 1440x900 option is on the screen resolution in system>preferences xorg is as follows

### Post by Rocket2DMn on 2008-04-26
Have you tried running
```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg -phigh
```
Then restart X with CTRL+ALT+BACKSPACE
Right now you are using the "vesa" fallback driver, which is really just a failsafe driver.   What is your video card?  If the above doesn't work, please post
```
lspci | grep VGA
```

---

### Post by Redbeard09 on 2008-04-26
That sees to have done the trick.

---

### Post by Julian Lewis on 2008-04-26

julian@LeazHome:~$ lspci |  grep VGA
01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: nVidia Corporation GeForce 8800 GS (rev a2)

I went back to the old xorg file, and got them working again, however I only have 1152 X 864.

Is there anyway I can get more resolution and compiz workingn with my driver ?

### Post by Rocket2DMn on 2008-04-26
Julian, you are using an nvidia card, so you may want to install the restricted drivers with EnvyNG - [http://albertomilone.com/envyngfaq.html#A](http://albertomilone.com/envyngfaq.html#A) - this will help you get the correct restricted drivers.

---

### Post by Julian Lewis on 2008-04-27
Looked good, but when I tried to run EnvyNG with automatic hardware detection under Ubuntu 8.04 on my system the package crashed, and I sent a bug report. It looks like this isn't working for my card on 8.04, ... or I made some mistake. Difficult to imagine what I did wrong though, I launched it from applications->System Tools->EnvyNG and chose NVIDIA. It then promptly crashed, nothing in /var/log/messages. Where can I look to see what went wrong ?
Julian

---

### Post by Rocket2DMn on 2008-04-27
OK, let's uninstall the driver, then remove envy and reinstall it
Open EnvyNG, click NVIDIA and select to Uninstall the NVIDIA drver.  Now close Envy, and run in a terminal:
```
sudo apt-get remove --purge envyng
sudo rm -R /usr/share/envy
sudo apt-get install envyng-gtk
```
Now try again to install the drivers with Envy.

---

### Post by Julian Lewis on 2008-05-03
It still crashes ...oks like my card isn too recent.
Julian


    task.drivertype()#latest, middle, oldest driver
  File "/usr/lib/python2.5/site-packages/Envy/classes.py", line 789, in drivertype
    raise Exception(error)
Exception: EnvyNG ERROR: Envy does not recognise your card as compatible with any version of the driver.this might happen because either your card is not supported by the driver or Envy's hardwaredetection failed. You can try the manual installation at your risk.
root@LeazHome:/usr/share/envy#

---

### Post by Julian Lewis on 2008-05-03
So I took a look at the NVIDIA download and found that my card needs driver version 169.12. Then with Envy I forced the installation by avoiding autodetect and did a manual choice. This worked correctly, but after restarting my computer there were no changes in behavior. I still do not have a good screen resolution. 

Julian

---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2008-05-04
Try running from terminal:
> sudo nvidia-settings
if it comes up with not found load nvidia-settings from terminal:
> sudo apt-get install nvidia-settings
once loaded run sudo nvidia-settings again and select your resolution and refresh rate.

---

### Post by Julian Lewis on 2008-05-04
Thanks, that did the trick, I didn't know there was a nvidia settings tool.
Thanks for the help, now it works a treat.

---
